body.title: Dedicated Postgres test schema with per-session drop — unblocks SQLite removal
body.result: pg_test_schema session fixture creates isolated test_clan_<ts>/test_instance_<ts> schemas; make_home_proxy/make_local_proxy respect IGOR_HOME_SEARCH_PATH; all 11 affected test files updated to route direct psycopg2 connections through the test search_path; seeds also respect the override; 3986 tests pass
body.description:
Create a dedicated Postgres test schema (e.g. `test_isolation`) with per-session auto-drop. Migrate tests/conftest.py + every `Cortex(db_path)` fixture site to use this schema via IGOR_HOME_DB_URL pointing at a Postgres test schema. Unblocks T-remove-sqlite-fallback by eliminating tests' last reliance on the SQLite fallback.

**Affected files:** tests/conftest.py (add session-scoped CREATE SCHEMA / DROP SCHEMA fixture), plus grep-discovery for every test that calls Cortex(tmp_path / '*.db'). Update conftest docstring to document the schema lifecycle + how to opt-in.
**Design rules:** no-sqlite: new schema lives in existing Postgres DB — no SQLite, no new DB instance. oop-first: session fixture is function-scoped with pytest — functional shape is pytest-idiomatic. docs-in-code: conftest.py docstring names the schema + lifecycle. test-plan-or-why-not: applies.
**Scope boundary:** IN — test schema creation+drop + conftest migration + fixture callsite updates. OUT — refactoring individual test logic, changing what tests do, parallel-DB isolation (single schema, drop-per-session is the agreed shape).
**Test plan:** Meta-test — assert the schema exists + is empty at session start. Grep-assert no remaining `Cortex(tmp_path` or `\.db` fixture references in tests/ after migration. Full test suite passes against Postgres.
